seoTitle: How to Create a Form in Confluence (2026)
metaDescription: Build a data collection form directly inside a Confluence page using the slash command, then track responses and set up notification emails.
intro: Instead of routing your team to a separate survey tool, Confluence lets you build a form directly on a page and collect responses without ever leaving your documentation. This guide covers building the form, setting up notification emails, and reviewing submitted responses.

steps.heading: Start the form macro
steps.body: Open an active Confluence page, enter edit mode, and type the /form command.

steps.heading: Create the form container
steps.body: Click Create Form to establish the main framework and name your target field.

steps.heading: Add your fields
steps.body: Use the right-hand canvas options panel to add dropdowns, checkboxes, and radio buttons to your form.

steps.heading: Set up conditional logic
steps.body: Configure conditional paths to show or hide specific questions dynamically based on earlier answers.

steps.heading: Configure notification emails
steps.body: Move to settings and input routing email addresses so your team gets notified the moment someone submits a response.

steps.heading: Publish and track responses
steps.body: Save the macro block, hit publish, and use the apps dashboard to audit submissions and view live response charts.

faq.question: Can I make a Confluence form field required?
faq.answer: Yes, when structuring your fields you can mark specific questions as required so submissions can't go through without them.

faq.question: Where do I see form responses after people submit them?
faq.answer: You can access the specialized apps dashboard to audit submissions chronologically without cluttering the main article page.

faq.question: Can I get notified by email when someone submits the form?
faq.answer: Yes, the settings panel lets you input routing email addresses to trigger automatic notification alerts.

faq.question: Do I need a marketplace app to build forms in Confluence?
faq.answer: The /form slash command gives you native form-building tools, though marketplace apps like Forms for Confluence can add more advanced options.
